insight: Brazil's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup shows that the team was able to gain momentum in half of the breaks, with an average change of -0.100 minutes per break, indicating a slight loss of momentum overall. However, when Brazil was pressing before a break, they tended to lose more momentum, with an average change of -0.300 minutes per break, and were unable to regain it after the break. In contrast, when Brazil was not pressing before a break, they were able to maintain or slightly gain momentum, with an average change of +0.100 minutes per break, suggesting that the team may benefit from a more conservative approach leading up to a hydration break. This data suggests that Brazil's strategy leading up to a break can significantly impact their performance after the break.
